toolDescriptions.json: Format, validate, tree view, and convert JSON ↔ YAML/CSV/XML
toolDescriptions.url: Encode/decode URL components or full URLs
toolDescriptions.jwt: Decode JWT — header, payload, signature (no verification)
toolDescriptions.jwt-sign: Sign and verify HS256 JWTs with a secret
toolDescriptions.data-url: Create/parse Data URLs, MIME, and inline Base64
toolDescriptions.uuid: Generate UUID v4 / v7 / Nil, with batch support
toolDescriptions.hmac: Generate HMAC SHA-1/256/384/512 with HEX/Base64 output
toolDescriptions.qr: Generate QR codes and download PNG files
toolDescriptions.lorem: Generate Lorem ipsum text by paragraph / sentence / word
toolDescriptions.password: Generate random passwords + passphrases and measure strength
toolDescriptions.timestamp: Convert Unix timestamp ↔ date with auto s/ms detection
toolDescriptions.color: Convert HEX / RGB / HSL / HSV / color names with swatches
toolDescriptions.unit: Convert length, weight, and temperature units
toolDescriptions.string-escape: Escape/unescape strings for JS, JSON, HTML, Regex, URL
toolDescriptions.diff: Compare two text blocks by line or word
toolDescriptions.sql: Quickly format/minify SQL with basic keyword highlighting
toolDescriptions.beautifier: Format/minify basic HTML, CSS, and JS snippets
toolDescriptions.cidr: Calculate network, broadcast, range, and IP-in-CIDR matches
toolDescriptions.robots: Check robots.txt basics and extract sitemaps
toolDescriptions.user-agent: Parse browser, OS, and device from User-Agent strings
toolDescriptions.regex: Test regex flags with highlighted matches and capture groups
toolDescriptions.config: Convert ENV ↔ JSON and flatten config keys
toolDescriptions.curl: Build curl commands and test HTTP requests in the browser
toolDescriptions.postman-docs: Convert Postman collection JSON into folder-based Markdown docs

messages.app.tagline: Developer toolbox — runs 100% in your browser

messages.install.iosHint: On iPhone/iPad: tap Share, then Add to Home Screen.
messages.install.title: Save Uni Tools as an app
messages.install.desc: Open it quickly like a standalone app, from your Home Screen, with less browser chrome.
messages.install.available: Install is available on this device
messages.install.notAvailable: If no prompt appears, use your browser menu and choose Add to Home Screen / Install app.
messages.info.title: Info
messages.info.subtitle: Quick information about Uni Tools, app install, and legal pages.
messages.info.appDesc: You can save Uni Tools as an app and launch it quickly from your home screen. Tools run in the browser and favor local processing.
messages.info.installStep1Desc: The browser opens the install prompt when the device supports it.
messages.info.installStep2Desc: Uni Tools opens like a standalone app, without hunting for an old tab.
messages.info.installStep3Desc: If the floating prompt was dismissed, this page keeps the install action available.
messages.info.privacyDesc: Most processing happens directly in your browser. Tool input is not sent to a Uni Tools server.
messages.info.offlineDesc: The app shell is cached so it opens faster after the first visit, useful for repeated developer workflows.
messages.info.policyDesc: How Uni Tools handles data and local storage.
messages.policy.title: Privacy Policy
messages.policy.subtitle: How Uni Tools handles data when you use tools on tools.hihi.team.
messages.policy.scopeDesc: This policy applies to tools.hihi.team and the tools running inside Uni Tools. It focuses on data you enter into tools, interface preferences, app install state, and information you intentionally send through feedback channels.
messages.policy.localDesc: Tools are designed to process input in the browser. Features such as HTTP requests or URL imports only send data to endpoints you explicitly enter.
messages.policy.networkDesc: Some tools need network calls to complete a task, such as testing a request, loading content from a URL, or opening an external link. In those cases, data is sent to the address you enter or to browser services required for the action, not so Uni Tools can read your tool content.
messages.policy.storageDesc: UI preferences, pinned tools, local history, and install state may be stored in browser localStorage. You can remove them by clearing site data.
messages.policy.feedbackDesc: If you send feedback by email or copy a feedback request, information such as name, contact, and request details is used only to read, respond, and improve Uni Tools. Do not send secrets, production tokens, or sensitive data through feedback.
messages.policy.securityDesc: Even though most processing happens locally, you should be careful with credentials, private keys, long-lived tokens, and real customer data. Use sample data when possible and clear site data when using a shared device.
messages.policy.contactDesc: For privacy questions or deletion requests about feedback you sent, contact hello@tools.hihi.team.
messages.terms.title: Terms
messages.terms.subtitle: Terms for using Uni Tools on tools.hihi.team.
messages.terms.acceptanceDesc: By accessing or using Uni Tools, you agree to use the tools under these terms. If you do not agree, you should stop using the website and clear locally stored browser data if needed.
messages.terms.useDesc: You are responsible for data entered into tools and for how you use the results. Do not use the service for unlawful activity or to harm other systems.
messages.terms.outputDesc: The tools support technical tasks such as formatting, encoding, decoding, generating, or converting data. Output may depend on the input, browser, libraries, and current configuration. Review results before using them in production, official documents, or important workflows.
messages.terms.thirdPartyDesc: If you enter a URL, API endpoint, or open an external link, that interaction may be governed by the third party's own terms and policies. Uni Tools does not control the content, uptime, security, or responses of endpoints you choose to call.
messages.terms.noWarrantyDesc: Tools are provided as-is. Review outputs before using them in important environments.
messages.terms.liabilityDesc: Uni Tools is built as a developer utility set, not as legal, security, financial, or operations advice. You are responsible for decisions made with the results and for any impact caused by data or commands you run.
messages.terms.changesDesc: Uni Tools may update tools, UI, and terms to improve the product.
